vehicleProfile.notes: Standard mechanical AWD configuration. This is not Adventure, TRD Off-Road, or Torque Vectoring AWD.

components.oilSpecification: SAE 0W-16 full synthetic; Toyota permits 0W-20 only when 0W-16 is unavailable, then return to 0W-16 at the next change
components.engineOilCapacityWithFilter: Approximately 4.8 qt; verify final level on dipstick after warm-up and drain-back
components.oilFilter: Toyota cartridge-style filter element; confirm exact current OE part number by VIN at purchase
components.coolant: Toyota Super Long Life Coolant, pink premixed 50/50
components.awdSystem: Front transfer assembly plus rear differential; no Haldex service and no torque-vectoring rear clutch bleed procedure
components.tireLugNutTorque: 76 lb-ft; verify with current owner's manual or service information

services.name: Engine Oil & Filter Change
tools: Oil filter housing wrench sized for installed cartridge cap
10 mm socket for lower splash-shield access panel
materials: Approximately 4.8 qt SAE 0W-16 full-synthetic engine oil
VIN-confirmed Toyota cartridge oil-filter element with new O-rings
steps: Bring the engine near operating temperature, park level, set the parking brake, and let hot oil cool enough to work safely.
Remove the lower splash-shield access panel as needed and position a drain pan.
Remove the drain plug and old crush washer; allow oil to drain completely.
Remove the cartridge housing carefully, replace the filter element and both supplied O-rings in their correct grooves, and lubricate O-rings with clean oil.
Install a new crush washer and torque the drain plug to the factory specification.
Refill with approximately 4.8 qt of 0W-16, start briefly, inspect for leaks, shut off, allow drain-back, and set the final dipstick level.
Reset the maintenance reminder through the instrument-cluster vehicle settings.
services.tips: Use 0W-16 as the normal fill. Do not substitute high-mileage 5W-30 simply because the vehicle has mileage; it is not the specified viscosity for this engine.
services.whatItDoes: Replaces degraded engine oil and the filter element that remove contaminants from the lubrication system.
services.whyItMatters: Clean oil protects the timing-chain system, variable valve timing hardware, bearings, and the variable-displacement oil-pump system.
services.ifDelayed: Old oil oxidizes and deposits sludge in small oil passages, increasing timing-system, bearing, and oil-consumption risk.

services.name: Engine Air Filter
materials: Toyota 17801-F0050 or VIN-confirmed equivalent engine air filter
steps: Locate the engine air box on the driver side of the engine bay.
Release the metal retaining clips and lift the cover from its rear locating tabs.
Remove the old element and vacuum loose debris from the lower housing without pushing debris into the intake tube.
Install the new filter with its sealing edge fully seated.
Re-engage rear tabs and clips, then verify that the air-box cover sits flat.
services.tips: Replace early after travel on gravel roads, construction sites, or dusty camping roads. A visually loaded filter is more meaningful than blindly stretching an interval.
services.whatItDoes: Filters incoming air before it enters the intake manifold and cylinders.
services.whyItMatters: An improperly seated or dirty filter can reduce airflow and allow abrasive dirt into the engine.
services.ifDelayed: Restriction can reduce performance and fuel economy; a poor seal can allow unfiltered dirt to accelerate cylinder and intake wear.

services.name: Cabin Air Filter
materials: Toyota 87139-series cabin filter or VIN-confirmed equivalent
steps: Empty and open the glove box.
Release the glove-box damper arm and squeeze the box sides inward to lower it.
Remove the rectangular cabin-filter cover behind the glove box.
Remove the old filter and observe the airflow or UP arrow printed on it.
Install the new filter in the same orientation, reinstall the cover, raise the glove box, and reconnect the damper.
services.tips: Follow the arrow printed on the new filter. Some filters label airflow direction while others label the physical UP direction.
services.whatItDoes: Filters air entering the passenger compartment through the HVAC system.
services.whyItMatters: A clean filter improves A/C and defrost airflow while reducing dust, pollen, and musty odors.
services.ifDelayed: Reduced HVAC airflow can compromise defrost performance and make the blower motor work harder.

services.name: 8-Speed Automatic Transmission Fluid Drain & Fill
tools: Vehicle scan tool capable of reading transmission-fluid temperature
Correct fill and drain tools for the installed transmission
Four level support points or a level lift
materials: VIN-confirmed replacement sealing washers or O-rings
steps: Confirm the vehicle is level and identify the fill plug before opening any drain or overflow plug.
Remove lower shields as required and drain the measured amount of fluid.
Reinstall the drain hardware with the required new seal and pump in the approximate measured quantity of Toyota ATF WS.
Use a scan tool to monitor transmission-fluid temperature and perform the factory-level-setting procedure within its specified temperature range.
With the engine running and transmission at the required temperature, open the level/overflow point and allow excess fluid to reduce to a thin controlled stream.
Install all plugs to factory torque, reinstall shields, road-test, and inspect for leaks.
services.tips: This transmission level is temperature-sensitive. Do not rely on a generic internet jumper-wire procedure when a scan tool can show actual transmission-fluid temperature.
services.whatItDoes: Refreshes a portion of the automatic transmission fluid that lubricates, cools, and hydraulically applies internal clutches.
services.whyItMatters: Fresh ATF helps preserve shift quality and valve-body/clutch durability.
services.ifDelayed: Degraded fluid can contribute to harsh shifts, delayed engagement, heat damage, and expensive internal transmission wear.

services.name: AWD Transfer Assembly & Rear Differential Fluid
materials: Toyota-approved gear oil meeting the exact viscosity/specification listed for this VIN
New aluminum crush washers for fill and drain plugs
steps: Raise and support the RAV4 level; remove underbody shielding only as needed.
Loosen the fill plug first on the front transfer assembly and the rear differential.
Drain each unit separately and inspect magnetic drain plugs for fine metallic paste; fine fuzz is normal, chunks are not.
Install drain plugs with new crush washers and torque them to the applicable factory specification.
Pump in the correct gear oil until it reaches the bottom of each fill opening and begins a slow weep.
Install fill plugs with new washers, clean both housings, and inspect later for seepage.
services.tips: Your XLE AWD uses standard AWD hardware. Do not add torque-vectoring-clutch or Adventure/TRD scan-tool bleed steps; they do not apply to this configuration.
services.whatItDoes: Replaces the gear lubricant in the front transfer assembly and rear differential.
services.whyItMatters: Gear oil protects hypoid gears, bearings, and AWD components under high contact pressure.
services.ifDelayed: Worn gear oil can lead to whining, bearing wear, gear damage, and costly AWD driveline repairs.

services.name: Brake Fluid Flush
steps: Protect painted surfaces and keep the master-cylinder reservoir from running low.
Use the factory bleed sequence for this vehicle and flush each caliper until new, clear fluid is visible.
Close each bleeder before releasing brake-pedal pressure.
Top off to the proper reservoir mark and verify a firm brake pedal before driving.
services.tips: If the pedal is soft or the ABS system has been opened, use the correct Toyota scan-tool ABS bleed procedure instead of assuming a normal manual bleed is sufficient.
services.whyItMatters: Brake fluid absorbs moisture, lowering its boiling point and promoting internal corrosion.
services.ifDelayed: Moisture can cause brake fade under repeated braking and damage expensive ABS hydraulic components.

services.name: Engine Coolant Renewal
materials: Toyota Super Long Life Coolant, pink premixed 50/50
steps: Work only on a completely cold engine.
Drain coolant through the designated radiator drain point into a suitable container.
Close the drain and refill with Toyota pink premixed coolant.
Bleed air using the factory procedure, operate the heater, allow the engine to cool fully, and recheck the reservoir level.
services.tips: Toyota pink Super Long Life Coolant is intended for long intervals. Do not mix coolant chemistries or use universal coolant without confirming compatibility.
services.whatItDoes: Renews coolant corrosion inhibitors and maintains freeze, boil, and heat-transfer protection.
services.whyItMatters: Cooling-system corrosion and degraded coolant can affect the water pump, radiator, heater core, and engine temperature control.
services.ifDelayed: Depleted corrosion inhibitors can contribute to water-pump, radiator, heater-core, and overheating problems.

services.name: Brake, Tire & Suspension Inspection
materials: None unless pads, rotors, tires, or suspension components are due
steps: Rotate tires using the rotation pattern appropriate for the tire type and tread design.
Torque all lug nuts to 76 lb-ft in a star pattern.
Measure pad thickness, inspect rotors, inspect caliper-slide boots, and check flexible brake hoses.
Inspect CV boots, front and rear suspension bushings, shocks, and wheel bearings.
Record tread depth at inner, center, and outer tread positions and schedule alignment if wear is uneven.
services.tips: Electric power steering can hide gradual alignment drift. Tread-depth measurements catch alignment or suspension problems before tires are ruined.
services.whatItDoes: Tracks brake wear, tire condition, alignment evidence, and common chassis wear points.
services.whyItMatters: These components directly affect traction, braking distance, steering response, and tire life.
services.ifDelayed: Ignoring uneven tire wear or low pads can turn inexpensive maintenance into tire, rotor, caliper, or suspension replacement.

services.name: Battery, Charging System & Terminal Inspection
materials: Replacement battery only if test results justify it
steps: Inspect battery case, hold-down, terminals, and cable ends for corrosion or looseness.
Measure resting voltage after the vehicle has been off long enough to stabilize.
Perform a load or conductance test and verify charging voltage with the engine running.
Disconnect negative first and positive second if cleaning is required; reconnect positive first and negative last.
services.tips: A replacement battery must match the correct physical group size, terminal layout, and original capacity requirement. Confirm by VIN and existing label before purchase.
services.whatItDoes: Checks battery health, cable condition, and alternator charging performance.
services.whyItMatters: Modern vehicles can generate multiple warning lights and driveability complaints from low system voltage.
services.ifDelayed: A weak battery can leave you stranded and cause misleading electrical or communication fault codes.
